task_completion.description: Google Earth application or website is successfully loaded
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Google Earth interface (globe, stars, or map view) is visible in a browser or application window.

task_completion.description: Agent searches for or navigates to Mount Everest
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search bar contains 'Mount Everest' or the location panel displays information about Mount Everest.

task_completion.description: The view is centered on Mount Everest
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main viewport shows a 3D rendering of a high snowy mountain peak consistent with Mount Everest.

task_completion.description: Agent accesses the built-in save or screenshot feature
task_completion.visual_evidence: A menu (e.g., File > Save > Save Image), toolbar icon, or overlay specific to saving/exporting an image within the Google Earth UI is visible/active.

task_completion.description: Image file is saved to the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new image file icon is visible on the Desktop wallpaper, or a file explorer window shows the image saved in the Desktop folder, or a browser download bar indicates a completed download to the Desktop.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands to navigate or capture the screen
integrity.visual_evidence: No command line interface (terminal, powershell) is being used to execute navigation or screenshot commands (e.g., `scrot`, `screencapture`).

integrity.description: Agent used the app's feature, not an OS-level screenshot tool
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshot action is triggered via the Google Earth interface, not by an OS-level overlay (like Snipping Tool, Win+Shift+S, or macOS screenshot crosshairs).

integrity.description: The file was generated by the application, not manually created
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is not seen creating a dummy file (e.g., right-click 'New File') or renaming an existing file to match the requirements.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target location is Mount Everest. The output file must be located on the Desktop. The agent is required to use the 'Save Image' or equivalent built-in feature of Google Earth, rather than an operating system screenshot tool.
